tick: A18 v3 wire — hashed order-3 byte-context range coder

v3_design.model: hashed order-3 byte-context adaptive range coder
v3_design.hash: Knuth multiplicative (mul 2654435761) of last 3 emitted tokens, mod 4096
v3_design.approach: non-PPM-D simplified bucketed hash variant — collisions = implicit context blending, byte-eq-safe by encoder/decoder mirror
v3_design.deviation_from_full_ppm_d: no explicit escape symbol; no per-context exclusion; 4096 buckets fold 256^3=16.7M raw contexts to keep memory bounded under hexa-lang map runtime

fixtures_added: fixture-10 v3 wire English-text byte-eq + saving% report (1000B)

aot_build.f10_compat: post-4b2918ec PASS — codegen_c2 bare-call map_* builtins handle v3 paths

comparison_table_v2_vs_v3.honest_c3: v3 LOST by 6.5pp; A23 v2 still winner-class

n6_atlas_79k.honest_c3: v3 hashed-bucket model attains ~50% of the H_3 theoretical bound on this corpus. Gap-to-theory dominated by 4096-bucket warm-up amortization vs 30K-token signal. Full PPM-D with sparse contexts + escape symbol could in principle close another ~20-30pp at the cost of 1-3K LoC and unbounded memory; out-of-scope for this tick.

aot_cache_gc_79k.honest_c3: On JSON-heavy 79KB v3 is essentially at the H_3 ceiling; v2 (93.9%) actually exceeds it because JSON-heavy data has order-1 token-pair regularities (key-value boundaries) that the order-3 hash dilutes.

aggregate_6_repo_post_a18_v3.rationale: a18_encode min-of-4 dispatcher correctly rejects v3 on every measured corpus (it is never the shortest of {v1, v2, v3, identity}). Aggregate is therefore byte-identical to the v2-dispatcher baseline. raw 142 D2 try-revert HONORED — no regression introduced.
aggregate_6_repo_post_a18_v3.verdict: 80% TARGET NOT REACHED VIA A18 v3

FAIL — already near ceiling, v3 dilutes order-1 boundary signal.

per_class_4_4_status_post_v3.v3_status: FAIL — small-file warm-up cost worst on v3.

falsifier_status.F-A18-V3-3: PASS — 12+ round-trip byte-eq verifications across selftest fixtures + LIVE FIRE corpora; zero failures.
falsifier_status.F-A18-1: UNCHANGED from v2 ledger — corpus-class-bounded close still holds (text-heavy ≥16 KB hive_triad-class FULLY-DEPLOYABLE 92-94% via v1; binary/structured n6 atlas plateaus at 50% v1).

falsifier_status.F-A18-3: AOT PASS unchanged — all wires <500 ms/KB. v3 ~12% slower than v2 on 79KB (0.62s vs 0.58s); both well within budget.

raw_91_honest_c3_disclosures: v3 wire algorithmic per-class CEILING lift attempt — hashed order-3 byte-context model (4096 buckets × 512 entries) — MEASURED HONESTLY: did NOT beat v2 on any of the 4 measured corpus classes. Net regression across the matrix: -6.3pp (text-heavy), -1.0pp (JSON-heavy), -0.6pp (struct-audit), -6.5pp (small-file). Average -3.6pp.

Root cause analysis: 4096-bucket warm-up amortization needs ≥30K tokens per bucket on average to converge; 79KB inputs produce ≈30K total tokens, giving ≈7 tokens per bucket — insufficient for adaptive learning. The order-3 model has more statistical structure but worse small-sample inference at this scale.

Theoretical-vs-measured gap on n6 atlas 79KB: Shannon H_3 ≈ 1.3 bits/byte (~84% bit-level theoretical) vs v3 measured 42.1% byte-level → 41.9pp gap to theoretical bound. The gap is consistent with a model that has not converged given the available signal.

F-A18-V3-1 TRIPPED honestly on all 4 corpus classes — algorithmic close attempt FAILED. v3 is BUILT, byte-eq verified, and CORRECTLY rejected by a18_encode min-of-4 try-and-revert (raw 142 D2). Aggregate 6-repo unchanged at 76.07%; gap to 80% remains 3.93pp.

raw 142 D2 try-revert correctly honored — fixture-12 verifies the dispatch picks the shortest of {v1=178, v2=189, v3=193, identity=234} = 178 (v1) on the JSON fixture. Same dispatch behaviour observed across all LIVE FIRE corpora.
raw 18 self-host fixpoint preserved BY CONSTRUCTION — only stdlib edited; codegen_c2.hexa and hexa_v2 unchanged.
raw 137 cmix-ban observed — no neural mixer added; pure deterministic hashed-bucket adaptive range coder. Knuth multiplicative hash + adaptive frequency tables + integer-only arithmetic.
raw 65 + 68 idempotent — A18 v1 → v2 → v3 transition preserves encode(decode(x)) = x and decode(encode(y)) = y under dispatcher selection.

Structural finding: the gap-to-80% (3.93pp) is NOT closeable via order-N+1 byte-context dispatcher-only at the input sizes available in this corpus battery. a38cd46d A25 honest C3 verdict ("4pp gap is structurally hard via dispatcher-only — must move per-class CEILING") is RECONFIRMED — and additionally, this tick demonstrates that direct algorithmic order-N+1 lift is also blocked by warm-up amortization at ≤79KB inputs.
next_tick_proposal: Three orthogonal options remain: (a) full sparse PPM-D with explicit escape symbol + per-context exclusion (≫600 LoC, unbounded memory) — high risk, fully algorithmic; (b) cross-file dictionary pre-seed via A19 layer chain (already shipped — pair A18×A19 chain measurement) — proven path; (c) corpus-class-specific pre-trained dictionaries (raw 137 axis 80% Pareto extension) — engineering-heavy. Option (b) recommended next per the v2 ledger's same recommendation; the 80% close path is via cross-file dictionary chain rather than per-class CEILING lift.
